### alpha - 2026-05-13 15:10 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete and committed: JSON array text canonical spacing from `TestAppCompatibilityBasicSmoke`.
- Root cause: the plain `json_agg` formatter always used `", "` between array elements. The PostgreSQL-compatible behavior in this codebase is compact separators for scalar/non-raw aggregate values such as `json_agg(text)::text`, while preserved plain-`json` raw input text still needs PostgreSQL's spaced separators between raw JSON elements.
- Change: plain JSON aggregate array separators now choose `","` for normal values and `", "` when either neighboring element carries preserved raw plain-JSON text. Added focused formatter regression tests for compact scalar arrays and raw-JSON separator preservation.
- Clean verifier proof at committed `HEAD` `e8ab735b` plus alpha patch in `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/alpha-json-spacing-head.qV3Aqb` after parser rebuild and local `third_party/dolt` link: red `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestAppCompatibilityBasicSmoke$' -count=1 -v` failed with expected `["first","second"]` vs actual `["first", "second"]`; green `go test -vet=off ./server/expression -run '^TestJsonAggFormatPlainArray' -count=1`; green `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestAppCompatibilityBasicSmoke|TestJsonAggregatesPreserveJsonInputTextRepro|TestJsonAggDistinctRequiresJsonEqualityOperatorRepro|TestJsonObjectAggDistinctRequiresJsonEqualityOperatorRepro|TestJsonbAggDistinctDeduplicatesJsonbValuesRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.
- Shared checkout proof: `go test -vet=off ./server/expression -run '^TestJsonAggFormatPlainArray' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./server/expression -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestAppCompatibilityBasicSmoke|TestJsonAggregatesPreserveJsonInputTextRepro|TestJsonAggDistinctRequiresJsonEqualityOperatorRepro|TestJsonObjectAggDistinctRequiresJsonEqualityOperatorRepro|TestJsonbAggDistinctDeduplicatesJsonbValuesRepro)$' -count=1 -v`; `git diff --check -- server/expression/json_agg.go server/expression/json_agg_test.go`.
- Commit: `cf9e8b27 fix: format plain json aggregate arrays`.

### epsilon - 2026-05-13 15:05 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete: quoted COPY column targets from `TestCopy/tab_delimited_with_quoted_column_names`.
- Root cause: regular INSERT encodes PostgreSQL column identifiers to physical storage names, but COPY's generated INSERT stub kept raw quoted names like `Id`, so COPY lookup missed the encoded table schema.
- Fix: COPY FROM insert stubs encode column names before binding; generated-column COPY validation uses the same encoded lookup and decodes names in user-facing errors.
- Test strengthened: quoted COPY now reads the loaded `"Id"`, `"Code"`, `"Capital"`, and `"Name"` rows back to prove values land in the intended columns.
- Proof in clean verifier and shared checkout: `go test -vet=off ./server/ast ./server -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestCopy$/(tab_delimited_with_quoted_column_names|generated_column)$|^TestCopyFromGeneratedColumnErrorKeepsSessionUsableRepro$' -count=1 -v`.

### beta - 2026-05-13 15:00 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete: versioned view status/reset behavior from `TestDoltResetHardRemovesUncommittedViewRepro` and `TestDoltResetUnstagesViewDefinitionRepro`.
- Root cause: Dolt status saw both the internal `dolt_schemas` table delta and Doltgres' virtual view root-object delta for the same view definition change. After status reports the logical view row, one-argument soft `DOLT_RESET(<view>)` also needs to route to the backing `dolt_schemas` storage table because Dolt reset cannot reset an individual virtual view root object.
- Change: Doltgres' `dolt_status` adapter filters internal `dolt_schemas` rows, and the Dolt procedure wrapper rewrites single-argument `dolt_reset` calls that resolve to views to the matching schema's `dolt_schemas` table. Files touched: `server/tables/dtables/status.go`, `server/functions/dolt_procedures.go`.
- Clean verifier proof in `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/beta-view-reset-post-a9e4493c` at `a9e4493c` plus beta patch after parser rebuild and local `third_party/dolt` link: `betatool test ./server/tables/dtables ./server/functions -vet=off -run '^$' -count=1`; `betatool test ./testing/go -vet=off -run '^(TestDoltResetHardRemovesUncommittedViewRepro|TestDoltResetUnstagesViewDefinitionRepro)$' -count=1 -v`; `betatool test ./testing/go -vet=off -run '^(TestDoltResetHardRemovesUncommitted(Function|Procedure|View|MaterializedView|EnumType|CompositeType|Domain)Repro|TestDoltResetUnstages(Function|Procedure|View|MaterializedView|Sequence|Trigger)DefinitionRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.

### gamma - 2026-05-13 14:50 MST

- Lane complete and committed: prepared/cached plan result-type invalidation after `ALTER TABLE ... DROP COLUMN` from `TestAlterTable/Drop_Column`.
- Root cause: `RunScript` was sending no-bind assertions through pgx's default statement cache, so repeated text like `select * from test1;` reused a protocol-prepared result shape across DDL. That is correct behavior for explicit prepared statements, but not the intended independent-statement script harness semantics.
- Change: no-bind script assertions now pass `pgx.QueryExecModeExec`, avoiding pgx's statement cache while leaving bind-var assertions on the existing prepared path. Explicit SQL prepared behavior is still covered by `TestPreparedSelectStarRejectsChangedResultShapeRepro`.
- Proof before commit in shared checkout: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestAlterTable$/Drop_Column$' -count=1 -v`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestPreparedSelectStarRejectsChangedResultShapeRepro|TestPreparedPlanCacheSQLInvalidation)$' -count=1 -v`; `git diff --check -- testing/go/framework.go`.
- Post-commit focused proof after peer commits advanced `HEAD` to `af2717f2`: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestAlterTable$/Drop_Column$' -count=1 -v` passed.
- Commit: `7b46036a test: avoid statement cache in script assertions`. No alpha-owned full `./testing/go` run started. Shared `coop.md`, dirty `server/node/grant.go`, dirty generated/oracle test files, dirty `third_party/dolt`, and other peer files were left unstaged.

Avoiding active beta RLS files, gamma inherited NOT NULL files, alpha JSON files including dirty `server/types/json_document.go`, auditor oracle/manifest files, dirty `server/node/grant.go`, dirty `third_party/dolt`, and peer connection-handler/parser lanes.
- Current signal from `/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-full-testing-go-53320c83-timeout30m.jsonl`: `ARRAY['abc   ']::varchar(3)[]` assigned to a `VARCHAR(3)[]` column failed with `value too long for type varchar(3): out of range`; PostgreSQL allows truncating only trailing spaces for assignment casts.
- Red proof in clean verifier `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/delta-varchar-array-current2.5hBm0d` at `be94ad9b`: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestVarcharArrayTypmodTruncatesTrailingSpacesGuard$' -count=1 -v` failed on `ARRAY['abc   ']::varchar(3)[]` with `value too long for type varchar(3): out of range`.
- Root cause: coerced array elements use the registered string cast helper in `server/cast/utils.go`; its varchar/bpchar typmod branches rejected any over-length input and did not mirror PostgreSQL's rule that all-space overflow may be truncated.
- Fix: `handleStringCast` now checks the overflow suffix and only errors when it contains non-space characters, returning the truncated value for explicit-cast callers that intentionally ignore sizing errors.
- Green proof in the same current-head verifier with the delta patch: `go test -vet=off ./server/cast -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestVarcharArrayTypmodTruncatesTrailingSpacesGuard|TestVarcharTypmodTruncatesTrailingSpacesRepro|TestCharacterTypmodTruncatesTrailingSpacesRepro|TestCharacterArrayTypmodStoresPaddedElementsRepro|TestVarcharArrayAppendValidatesElementTypmodRepro|TestVarcharArrayPrependValidatesElementTypmodRepro|TestCharacterArrayAppendAppliesElementTypmodRepro|TestCharacterArrayPrependAppliesElementTypmodRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.
- Commit: `574e39ca fix: allow trailing-space varchar array casts`.
- Exact post-commit proof at `574e39ca` in `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/delta-varchar-array-post.0ja9PC` after parser build and pinned `third_party/dolt` link: `go test -vet=off ./server/cast -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestVarcharArrayTypmodTruncatesTrailingSpacesGuard|TestVarcharTypmodTruncatesTrailingSpacesRepro|TestCharacterTypmodTruncatesTrailingSpacesRepro|TestCharacterArrayTypmodStoresPaddedElementsRepro|TestVarcharArrayAppendValidatesElementTypmodRepro|TestVarcharArrayPrependValidatesElementTypmodRepro|TestCharacterArrayAppendAppliesElementTypmodRepro|TestCharacterArrayPrependAppliesElementTypmodRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.

- Red proof at `2d8e67b4` in `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/delta-generated-tableoid.H0iFSo`: `TestGeneratedColumnAllowsTableoidReferenceRepro` failed at CREATE TABLE setup with `column "tableoid" could not be found in any table in scope`; adjacent `TestSelectCanProjectTableoidRepro` failed with the same resolution error. `TestDmlReturningCanProjectTableoidRepro` already passed through the existing DML rewrite, and `TestGeneratedColumnRejectsSystemColumnReferencesRepro` still rejected `xmin`.
- Root cause: `tableoid` had only a connection-handler DML RETURNING rewrite. Normal SELECT and generated-column expressions arrive during AST conversion as `tree.UnresolvedName`, then resolve as ordinary user columns and fail because user-table schemas do not expose PostgreSQL system columns.
- Fix: AST conversion now tracks a single surrounding table relation for simple SELECT projections and CREATE/ALTER generated-column expressions, and lowers unqualified `tableoid` to that relation's `regclass` value. Unsupported system columns remain ordinary unresolved names and continue to reject.
- Green proof in the same verifier with the delta patch: `go test -vet=off ./server/ast -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestGeneratedColumnAllowsTableoidReferenceRepro|TestGeneratedColumnRejectsSystemColumnReferencesRepro|TestSelectCanProjectTableoidRepro|TestDmlReturningCanProjectTableoidRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.
- Commit: `bc363be0 fix: allow tableoid generated expressions`.
- Exact post-commit proof at `bc363be0` in `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/delta-generated-tableoid-post.DJMxnc` after local `third_party/dolt` link and parser build: `go test -vet=off ./server/ast -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestGeneratedColumnAllowsTableoidReferenceRepro|TestGeneratedColumnRejectsSystemColumnReferencesRepro|TestSelectCanProjectTableoidRepro|TestDmlReturningCanProjectTableoidRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.

### alpha - 2026-05-13 14:14 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete: JSON aggregate DISTINCT semantics from `TestJsonAggDistinctRequiresJsonEqualityOperatorRepro`, `TestJsonObjectAggDistinctRequiresJsonEqualityOperatorRepro`, and `TestJsonbAggDistinctDeduplicatesJsonbValuesRepro`.
- Red proof in clean verifier `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/alpha-json-distinct-e1bb9dfa` at committed `e1bb9dfa`: plain `json_agg(DISTINCT json)` and `json_object_agg(DISTINCT ..., json)` returned rows instead of PostgreSQL's `could not identify an equality operator for type json`; `jsonb_agg(DISTINCT jsonb)` returned duplicate values.
- Root cause: JSON aggregate DISTINCT keying used `%#v` over unwrapped values with no type-aware equality boundary, so plain `json` was treated as comparable and JSONB documents were keyed by Go shape rather than canonical JSONB text.
- Fix: JSON aggregate DISTINCT now rejects any plain `json` input expression and keys `jsonb` inputs by canonical `JsonValueFormatter` output. Generic aggregate DISTINCT keying used by `array_agg` remains on the existing fallback path.
- Files touched: `server/expression/json_agg.go`. Avoided active gamma/delta inheritance files, beta RLS files, epsilon parser/jsonpath files, auditor oracle/manifest files, dirty `server/node/grant.go`, dirty `third_party/dolt`, and alpha-owned full-suite runs.
- Clean verifier proof: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestJsonAggDistinctRequiresJsonEqualityOperatorRepro|TestJsonObjectAggDistinctRequiresJsonEqualityOperatorRepro|TestJsonbAggDistinctDeduplicatesJsonbValuesRepro)$' -count=1 -v`; `go test -vet=off ./server/expression -run '^$' -count=1`.
- Commit: `3fae295f fix: handle json aggregate distinct semantics`. Shared `coop.md` and peer dirty files left unstaged.
- Cleanup: removed alpha verifier cache `/Users/ramazan/.cache/doltgresql-alpha-json-distinct` and worktree `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/alpha-json-distinct-e1bb9dfa`.

### alpha - 2026-05-13 14:06 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete: plain `to_json(record)` field-order preservation from `TestToJsonRecordPreservesFieldOrderRepro`.
- Red proof in clean verifier `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/alpha-tojson-record-952d8184` at committed `952d8184`: `to_json(r)::text` returned `{"a":2,"b":1}` while PostgreSQL expects plain JSON record conversion to preserve field order as `{"b":1,"a":2}`; `row_to_json` already preserved order and `to_jsonb` correctly sorted keys.

- Root cause: the existing inherited read wrapper only ran for read-only plans in the normal analyzer flow; simple UPDATE/DELETE use GMS custom batches that skip the `OnceAfterDefault` inherited-scan insertion, DELETE targets are stored outside the normal child traversal, and the inherited wrapper did not implement writable/truncatable editors or map projected parent rows back to concrete child rows.

### delta - 2026-05-13 13:32 America/Phoenix

- Lane green in clean verifier: numeric typmod and special-value correctness for `TestNumericNegativeScaleRoundsStoredValuesRepro`, `TestNumericScaleGreaterThanPrecisionRoundsStoredValuesRepro`, and `TestNumericSpecialValuesRoundTripRepro`.
- Root causes: parser/type metadata rejected PostgreSQL 15+ numeric typmods with negative scale or scale greater than precision; typmod decode treated negative scale as unsigned; storage rounding/overflow checked string lengths before PostgreSQL-style rounding; numeric IO/comparison/storage assumed every `numeric` value was a finite `decimal.Decimal`, so `NaN`/`Infinity` could not parse or persist.
- Fix: allow signed numeric scales through parser/type metadata; sign-extend numeric typmod scale; round with `decimal.Round(scale)` and enforce `abs(value) < 10^(precision-scale)`; represent special numeric values as internal decimal sentinels so Dolt storage still receives `decimal.Decimal`, while IO/wire/comparison/serialization expose PostgreSQL `-Infinity < finite < Infinity < NaN` semantics and `NaN = NaN`.
- Red proof at current committed `HEAD` `6451db6b` in `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/delta-numeric-current.az7rtt`: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestNumericNegativeScaleRoundsStoredValuesRepro|TestNumericScaleGreaterThanPrecisionRoundsStoredValuesRepro|TestNumericSpecialValuesRoundTripRepro)$' -count=1 -v` failed with `NUMERIC(2, -3)` syntax error, `NUMERIC(3, 5)` scale rejection, and `invalid input syntax for type numeric: "NaN"`.
- Green proof in same verifier after patch and `./postgres/parser/build.sh`: `go test -vet=off ./server/types ./server/functions/binary ./server/cast -run '^TestNumeric' -count=1 -v`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestNumericNegativeScaleRoundsStoredValuesRepro|TestNumericScaleGreaterThanPrecisionRoundsStoredValuesRepro|TestNumericSpecialValuesRoundTripRepro)$' -count=1 -v`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestNumeric' -count=1 -v`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestTypes$/(Numeric type|Numeric type, no scale or precision|Arbitrary precision types)$' -count=1 -v`.
- Commit: `ec1df6dd fix: support numeric typmod and special values`.
- Exact post-commit proof at latest committed `HEAD` `90febcf2` in `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/delta-numeric-head.f9tv0s` after `./postgres/parser/build.sh` and local `third_party/dolt` worktree: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestNumericNegativeScaleRoundsStoredValuesRepro|TestNumericScaleGreaterThanPrecisionRoundsStoredValuesRepro|TestNumericSpecialValuesRoundTripRepro)$' -count=1 -v`; `go test -vet=off ./server/types ./server/functions/binary ./server/cast -run '^TestNumeric' -count=1 -v`.
- Next action: numeric lane complete; shared `coop.md`, peer `server/node/drop_sequence.go` and `server/node/grant.go`, and dirty `third_party/dolt` remain unstaged unless explicitly requested.

### alpha - 2026-05-13 12:17 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete: plain `json` object order/duplicate-key preservation for `TestJsonEachPreservesJsonObjectOrderAndDuplicatesRepro` and `TestJsonObjectKeysPreservesJsonObjectOrderAndDuplicatesRepro`.
- Root cause: plain `json` function input was decoded through `json.Unmarshal` into `map[string]interface{}`, so object field order and duplicate keys were lost before `json_each` / `json_object_keys` iterated the value. `jsonb` should keep the old canonical path.
- Fix: added a plain-json ordered decoder that preserves object item order and duplicate fields, and routed `pgtypes.Json` function parsing through it while leaving `jsonb` on `UnmarshalToJsonDocument`.
- Commit: `8c2db815 fix: preserve plain json object iteration order`.
- Green proof: in clean verifier `/Users/ramazan/Code/oss/doltgresql-verifiers/alpha-json-post-8c2db815` after `./postgres/parser/build.sh` and local `third_party/dolt` link, `go test -vet=off ./server/types -run '^Test(UnmarshalToJsonDocumentPreserveObjectItems|JsonDocumentString|JsonStringUnescape)$' -count=1 -v`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestJsonEachPreservesJsonObjectOrderAndDuplicatesRepro|TestJsonObjectKeysPreservesJsonObjectOrderAndDuplicatesRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.
- Notes: shared checkout was build-broken during proof by unrelated active RLS edits, so alpha used detached verifier proof. Also repaired the local Go 1.26.2 module toolchain after cache cleanup left it incomplete.
- Next action: cleanup alpha verifier caches/worktrees, then refresh current lanes before claiming another isolated failure.

### alpha - 2026-05-13 11:58 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete locally: deferrable unique and primary-key timing from `TestDeferrableUniqueConstraintCanBeFixedBeforeCommitRepro`, `TestDeferrablePrimaryKeyConstraintCanBeFixedBeforeCommitRepro`, and `TestSetConstraintsDefersUniqueConstraintRepro`.
- Root cause: DEFERRABLE unique/PK declarations were parsed incompletely for table constraints and still lowered to native Dolt unique/primary indexes, so duplicate rows were rejected before transaction-local DEFERRABLE timing could apply.
- Fix: unique/PK deferrability is preserved in parser/index metadata, deferrable unique/PK constraints lower to metadata-backed non-unique btree indexes, DML wraps those tables with transaction-aware immediate/deferred validation, and COMMIT/SET CONSTRAINTS IMMEDIATE run deferred duplicate scans. Catalog/replica-identity paths now use PostgreSQL-facing metadata uniqueness/deferrability.

- Lane complete: quoted column identifier/catalog case sensitivity from `TestQuotedColumnNamesAreCaseSensitiveRepro`.

Avoided active publication/subscription files, grant/oracle files, and `third_party/dolt`.
- Red proof: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestQuotedColumnNamesAreCaseSensitiveRepro$' -count=1 -v` first failed at setup with `duplicate column name: casecolumn`; after exact-name CREATE TABLE validation it failed on INSERT with `column "casecolumn" specified more than once`.
- Root cause: GMS validates and resolves column lists case-insensitively before Doltgres analyzer rules run, so quoted mixed-case columns must use distinct physical names before reaching GMS while keeping PostgreSQL-facing catalog names.
- Fix: mixed-case/prefix-conflicting PostgreSQL column names are encoded to deterministic physical GMS names in CREATE TABLE, INSERT target lists, and column references; `pg_attribute` and `information_schema.columns` decode them for catalog output; CREATE TABLE duplicate validation now checks exact parsed names.
- Green proof: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestQuotedColumnNamesAreCaseSensitiveRepro$' -count=1 -v`; `go test -vet=off ./core ./server/ast ./server/analyzer ./server/tables/pgcatalog ./server/tables/information_schema -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestQuotedColumnNamesAreCaseSensitiveRepro|TestQuotedTableNamesAreCaseSensitiveRepro|TestQuotedMaterializedViewNamesAreCaseSensitiveRepro|TestQuotedSequenceNamesAreCaseSensitive)$' -count=1 -v`.
- Lane committed: `efcfe3b5 fix: preserve quoted column names`.
- Next action: clean alpha quoted-column cache dirs, then refresh status before another lane.

- Red proof at current dirty shared checkout: focused quoted identifier run still failed table/materialized-view catalog rows because `pg_class` only returned the folded lower-case relation name even though exact root names existed.
- Root cause: Doltgres relation iteration enumerated exact root table names, but then rebuilt table OIDs and `pg_class.relname` from the resolved GMS table object; that object can normalize case when case-differing names coexist.
- Fix: preserve exact root table names in `functions.ItemTable.OID`, supplement `Database.GetTableNames` with exact root names for case-differing relations, and use the exact table OID name for `pg_class.relname` / owner / replica identity.
- Green proof: `go test -vet=off ./server/functions ./server/tables ./server/tables/pgcatalog -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestQuotedTableNamesAreCaseSensitiveRepro|TestQuotedMaterializedViewNamesAreCaseSensitiveRepro|TestSequenceRelationTracksIsCalledRepro|TestUnloggedSequenceRelpersistenceCatalogMetadataRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.

### alpha - 2026-05-13 09:46 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete: sequence relation table lookup from `TestSequenceRelationTracksIsCalledRepro`.
- Files touched: `server/connection_handler.go` only. Avoided active epsilon comment cleanup files, delta routine validation files, dirty grant/RLS files, oracle files, and Alpha-owned full `./testing/go`.
- Current focused proof after `1adff18b`: adding this test to the identity cluster failed on `SELECT last_value, is_called FROM sequence_is_called_target` with `table not found`, while `SELECT nextval('sequence_is_called_target')` passed.
- Root cause: connection-local extended query binding could still use the running engine before its catalog provider had been wrapped with Doltgres' sequence-aware provider. Auth/session probes could find the sequence, but the actual table scan saw only Dolt tables.
- Fix: idempotently wrap the running engine catalog provider in `NewConnectionHandler` before constructing the connection's `DoltgresHandler`.
- Green proof with isolated alpha cache/temp dirs: `go test -vet=off ./server ./server/tables -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestSequenceRelationTracksIsCalledRepro|TestIdentitySequenceOptionsAffectGeneratedValuesRepro|TestAlterColumnIdentitySequenceOptionsRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.

- Lane complete: identity sequence option behavior from `TestIdentitySequenceOptionsAffectGeneratedValuesRepro`.
- Files touched: `server/ast/column_table_def.go`, `server/ast/create_table.go`, `server/analyzer/serial.go`, and `server/node/identity_sequence_options.go`. Avoided dirty grant/RLS and oracle manifest files.

- Root cause: `nodeColumnTableDef` rejected all identity `Computed.Options`, so parsed `START WITH` / `INCREMENT BY` options never reached `ReplaceSerial`, which creates the owned identity sequence.
- Fix: carry parsed identity sequence options through a Doltgres-only CREATE TABLE option and apply them to the generated sequence when `ReplaceSerial` builds the backing sequence.
- Green proof with isolated alpha cache/temp dirs: `go test -vet=off ./server/analyzer ./server/ast ./server/node -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestIdentitySequenceOptionsAffectGeneratedValuesRepro|TestAlterColumnIdentitySequenceOptionsRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.

- Red proof in shared checkout at `HEAD` `00511ea1`: view and materialized-view comments persisted, but `COMMENT ON SEQUENCE sequence_comment_target IS 'visible sequence comment'` left `obj_description('sequence_comment_target'::regclass)` NULL.
- Root cause: `COMMENT ON SEQUENCE` stored the comment under the internal sequence ID, while `regclass` and `pg_class.oid` expose the sequence as a relation OID. `DROP SEQUENCE` also removed only the sequence-ID comment key.
- Fix: resolve sequence comment targets through the pg_class relation path and remove both old sequence-ID and relation-ID comment keys on drop.
- Green proof with isolated epsilon cache/temp dirs and pinned Go 1.26.2/ICU env: `go test -vet=off ./server/node -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestCommentOnRelationKindsPersistsDescriptionRepro|TestDropSequenceClearsCommentRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.

- Red proof in shared checkout at current dirty `HEAD` `04e8e15a`: `TestCompositeTypeDomainFieldEnforcesConstraintsRepro` failed because `CREATE TYPE ... (amount composite_field_positive)` stored an unresolved type id and inserts later reported `type "composite_field_positive" does not exist`; `TestCompositeDomainAcceptsValidValuesRepro` panicked while building `(VALUE).x` checks for a domain over a composite type.
- Root cause: composite type attributes were not resolved to schema-qualified user-defined type IDs at `CREATE TYPE` execution time; composite field access treated domains over composite types as non-composite; composite casts did not validate domain-typed attributes; and explicit domain casts resolved the base type for lookup but still passed the domain type into the cast function.
- Fix in progress: resolve composite attribute types during `CREATE TYPE`, unwrap domain-over-composite types for column access, attach domain checks to composite cast attributes, and cast explicit domain values through the resolved base type before validating the domain.
- Green proof: `go test -vet=off ./server/analyzer ./server/node ./server/expression -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestCompositeDomainAcceptsValidValuesRepro|TestCompositeTypeDomainFieldEnforcesConstraintsRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.

Avoiding active delta JSON-key files, epsilon pattern-match files, gamma logical-replication row-filter files, dirty grant/RLS files, auditor oracle files, and beta transaction/read-only files.
- Red proof in clean detached verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-arrset-red-aaJ8IE` at committed `HEAD` `98f5be78`: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestArraySubscriptAssignmentPersistsElementRepro$' -count=1 -v` fails because `UPDATE array_subscript_assignment_items SET values_int[2] = 22 WHERE id = 1` errors at parse time with `at or near "[": syntax error`, leaving the stored array as `{1,2,3}` instead of `{1,22,3}`.
- Root cause: the UPDATE grammar only accepts bare `column_name = expr` targets, and GMS `SetField` only updates normal column `GetField` targets. Array element assignment therefore needs to parse `column[index]` but lower it as a whole-column assignment whose RHS computes the updated array from the original column value.
- Fix in progress: added a narrow parser/update AST path for one-dimensional `column[index] = expr` and a Doltgres `ArraySetElement` expression used by `server/ast/update_expr.go` to emit `column = array_set_element(column, index, expr)`.
- Shared green proof with alpha-private cache/temp dirs: `go test -vet=off ./postgres/parser/sem/tree ./server/ast ./server/expression -run '^$' -count=1`; `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestArraySubscriptAssignmentPersistsElementRepro|TestArrayLiteralPreservesLowerBoundsRepro|TestArrayLowerReportsDefaultLowerBoundRepro|TestArrayDimsReportsDimensionsRepro)$' -count=1 -v`.

### delta - 2026-05-13 07:42 America/Phoenix

- Lane committed: `7c628d89 fix: resolve unqualified sequence relations`, for unqualified sequence relation lookup from `TestSequenceRelationTracksIsCalledRepro`.
- Files touched by delta: `server/server.go`, `server/tables/database.go`, and `server/tables/database_provider.go`. Avoided active alpha window files, beta range files, gamma logical-replication files, epsilon string-comparison files, dirty grant/RLS files, and auditor oracle files.
- Root cause: unqualified plan building uses the catalog provider's current database directly, so it bypassed Doltgres' schema wrapper where virtual sequence tables are exposed. Schema-qualified sequence scans worked because `GetSchema("public")` went through `sqle.HandleSchema`.

- Root cause: the GMS window implementations only cover literal `lag/lead` offsets and partition-level `ntile` arguments, while Doltgres parser output aliases constants before GMS validates them; `nth_value` is absent from GMS's recognized window function list.
- Fix in progress: route PostgreSQL value window functions through Doltgres's internal window hook and implement frame-aware `nth_value`, row-aware dynamic `lag/lead` offsets/defaults, and row-aware `ntile` bucket counts under `server/expression`.

- Red proof in clean detached verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-indexexpr-red-bOky2Y` at committed `HEAD` `6f216f62`: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestIndexDefinitionsRejectInvalidExpressionsRepro$' -count=1 -v` fails because Doltgres accepts invalid SRF, volatile, stable, UDF volatile/stable, and subquery index definitions; aggregate expression is rejected only accidentally as a missing synthetic column.
- Root cause found: `validateIndex` validates index columns/opclasses but does not validate PostgreSQL expression classes or the encoded partial-index predicate; created routine metadata also records every SQL/PLPGSQL function as non-deterministic without consulting `VOLATILITY`.
- Patch in progress: add index expression/predicate class validation in `server/analyzer/validate_create_table.go` and make `CREATE/ALTER FUNCTION` synchronize `IsNonDeterministic` with `VOLATILITY`. Package compile is rerunning with an isolated cache after the shared Go build cache was found stale.

### beta - 2026-05-13 07:00 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete: `DOLT_BRANCH(...)` inside the Go test harness savepoint wrapper, starting from the branch-qualified definition repro cluster.
- Fix committed on `main`: `87c9454a fix: preserve statement savepoints across branch calls`.
- Expected files: branch procedure / transaction handling paths only; avoiding active alpha generated-column files, epsilon builtin-array catalog files, delta JSON/array files, gamma logical-replication files, dirty grant/RLS files, and alpha-owned full `./testing/go`.
- Red proof in shared checkout at `HEAD` `7cd2b759` with isolated cache/temp dirs and explicit ICU flags: focused `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestBranchQualifiedCompositeTypeDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedDomainDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedEnumTypeDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedFunctionDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedProcedureDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedSequenceDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedSerialTableCreatesSequenceRepro)$' -count=1 -v` fails every case during setup on `SELECT DOLT_BRANCH('...')` with `SAVEPOINT __doltgresql_statement_N does not exist`.
- Root cause: Dolt versioning functions such as `dolt_branch` commit the current transaction and start a replacement transaction inside the function call. The Doltgres context-root finalizer created a statement savepoint on the old transaction, then attempted to release it on the replacement transaction and surfaced `SAVEPOINT __doltgresql_statement_N does not exist`.
- Fix: remember the transaction used for the statement savepoint, and skip statement-savepoint rollback/release if the child node has crossed a transaction boundary.
- Green proof in shared checkout plus beta patch with isolated cache/temp dirs and explicit ICU flags:
  - `go test -vet=off ./server/node -run '^$' -count=1`
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestDoltBranch|TestDoltCheckout|TestSavepoints|TestBranchQualifiedCompositeTypeDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedDomainDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedEnumTypeDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedFunctionDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedProcedureDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedSequenceDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedSerialTableCreatesSequenceRepro)$' -count=1 -v`
- Exact post-commit proof in clean detached verifier `/private/tmp/beta-branch-post-zG9Gee` at `87c9454a` after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`, symlinked `third_party/dolt`, reusable cache (`GOCACHE=/Users/ramazan/.cache/beta-branch-post-gocache`, `GOTMPDIR=/Users/ramazan/.cache/beta-branch-post-gotmp`), `GOFLAGS='-p=1'`, and explicit ICU flags:
  - `go test -vet=off ./server/node -run '^$' -count=1`
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestDoltBranch|TestDoltCheckout|TestSavepoints|TestBranchQualifiedCompositeTypeDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedDomainDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedEnumTypeDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedFunctionDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedProcedureDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedSequenceDefinitionRepro|TestBranchQualifiedSerialTableCreatesSequenceRepro)$' -count=1 -v`

### alpha - 2026-05-13 06:59 America/Phoenix

- Lane green pre-commit: generated-column expression metadata during `ALTER ... SET/DROP NOT NULL` from `TestAlterGeneratedColumnSetNotNullEnforcesGeneratedValueRepro` and `TestAlterGeneratedColumnDropNotNullPreservesGeneratedValueRepro`.
- Files touched: `server/analyzer/validate_column_defaults.go` only. Avoided active/dirty peer JSON, grant, window, array/domain, logical-replication, and materialized-view oracle files.
- Red proof in clean detached verifier at committed `HEAD` `101ad841` before the alpha patch: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestAlterGeneratedColumnSetNotNullEnforcesGeneratedValueRepro|TestAlterGeneratedColumnDropNotNullPreservesGeneratedValueRepro)$' -count=1 -v` failed both tests with `Invalid default value for '(nullif(...,0 as __doltgres_anon__...))': at or near "as": syntax error`.
- Root cause: resolved generated-column/default expressions can carry internal `expression.Alias` projection wrappers. Those wrappers leaked into `ColumnDefaultValue.String()` and were later reparsed/persisted as invalid SQL during ALTER metadata rewrites. For `ModifyColumn`, the generated expression in `NewColumn()` is separate from the target schema defaults, so sanitizing only the target schema still left ALTER SET/DROP NOT NULL with stale generated metadata.
- Fix: strip internal aliases from `ColumnDefaultValue` expressions during default validation, preserve generated-column classification before rebuilding the default, and keep `ModifyColumn.NewColumn().Generated` synchronized with the sanitized generated expression.
- Green proof in clean detached verifier at current `HEAD` `d335df6c` plus only the alpha patch after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`, symlinked `third_party/dolt`, isolated cache/temp dirs, `GOFLAGS='-p=1'`, and explicit ICU flags:
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestAlterGeneratedColumnSetNotNullEnforcesGeneratedValueRepro|TestAlterGeneratedColumnDropNotNullPreservesGeneratedValueRepro|TestGeneratedColumnRecomputesAfterBaseUpdateGuard|TestGeneratedColumnNotNullConstraintRepro)$' -count=1 -v`
  - `go test -vet=off ./server/analyzer -run '^$' -count=1`
- Result: committed as `880d5def fix: sanitize generated column defaults`.
- Exact post-commit proof in clean detached verifier at `880d5def` after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`, symlinked `third_party/dolt`, isolated cache/temp dirs, `GOFLAGS='-p=1'`, and explicit ICU flags:
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestAlterGeneratedColumnSetNotNullEnforcesGeneratedValueRepro|TestAlterGeneratedColumnDropNotNullPreservesGeneratedValueRepro|TestGeneratedColumnRecomputesAfterBaseUpdateGuard|TestGeneratedColumnNotNullConstraintRepro)$' -count=1 -v`
  - `go test -vet=off ./server/analyzer -run '^$' -count=1`
- Next action: clean the exact verifier worktree/cache and refresh current failures/lanes before claiming another alpha slice.

### beta - 2026-05-13 06:48 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete: `avg(...) FILTER (...) OVER (...)` empty-frame result from `TestAvgWindowAggregateFilterReturnsNullForEmptyFrameRepro`.
- Fix committed on `main`: `d335df6c fix: return null for empty avg window frames`.
- Files touched: `server/expression/gms_cast.go`. Avoided active epsilon array/domain files, alpha generated-column files, auditor materialized-view oracle files, gamma logical-replication files, dirty JSON/grant files, and alpha-owned full `./testing/go`.
- Red proof in clean detached verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-window2-red-AH005c` at committed `HEAD` `fea7eff7`: focused `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestCumeDistWindowFunctionRepro|TestAvgWindowAggregateFilterReturnsNullForEmptyFrameRepro)$' -count=1 -v` fails the avg-filter lane with `value NaN is not a valid Decimal`. The cume_dist failure from that same run is already fixed by beta commit `4f2ef147`.
- Root cause: PostgreSQL aggregate `FILTER` is rewritten to a NULL-producing `CASE` expression; GMS window `AVG` divides by the non-NULL count and returns NaN for frames where that rewritten child is all NULL, which Doltgres then fails to cast to numeric.
- Fix: before casting a GMS window `AVG` result, inspect the current frame's avg child expression; if every value is NULL, return PostgreSQL's NULL result instead of asking GMS to compute and cast NaN.
- Green proof in clean detached verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-avgwindow-green-0KTYn4` at committed `HEAD` `d1d4d211` plus only the beta patch after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`, symlinked `third_party/dolt`, isolated cache (`GOCACHE=/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-avgwindow-gocache-utTDiX`, `GOTMPDIR=/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-avgwindow-gotmp-UX0pFr`), `GOFLAGS='-p=1'`, and explicit ICU flags:
  - `go test -vet=off ./server/expression -run '^$' -count=1`
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestAvgWindowAggregateFilterReturnsNullForEmptyFrameRepro|TestWindowFunctions)$' -count=1 -v`
- Exact post-commit proof in clean detached verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-avgwindow-post-ywnRY0` at `d335df6c` after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`, symlinked `third_party/dolt`, isolated cache (`GOCACHE=/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-avgwindow-post-gocache-KiKg3x`, `GOTMPDIR=/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-avgwindow-post-gotmp-6tXtjN`), `GOFLAGS='-p=1'`, and explicit ICU flags:

- Red proof in clean detached verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-window2-red-AH005c` at committed `HEAD` `fea7eff7`: focused `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestCumeDistWindowFunctionRepro|TestAvgWindowAggregateFilterReturnsNullForEmptyFrameRepro)$' -count=1 -v` fails `cume_dist()` with `function: 'cume_dist' not found; function does not exist`. The same run also confirms the independent `avg(...) FILTER` empty-frame Decimal NaN failure, which is not this lane.
- Root cause: like the bool window aggregates, `cume_dist` is not in GMS's hardcoded `isWindowFunc` list, so `cume_dist() OVER (...)` never reaches window planning.
- Fix: route parsed `cume_dist() OVER (...)` calls through Doltgres's internal window hook with a cume marker, then compute PostgreSQL `cume_dist` from the current peer-group interval as rows through the current peer group divided by partition row count.
- Green proof in clean detached verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-cumedist-green-kU0kWI` at committed `HEAD` `101ad841` plus only the beta patch after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`, symlinked `third_party/dolt`, isolated cache (`GOCACHE=/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-cumedist-gocache-QUhcH0`, `GOTMPDIR=/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-cumedist-gotmp-Q02N3z`), `GOFLAGS='-p=1'`, and explicit ICU flags:

  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestCumeDistWindowFunctionRepro|TestBooleanAggregatesCanBeWindowFunctionsRepro|TestArrayAggCanBeWindowFunctionRepro)$' -count=1 -v`

- Fix committed on `main`: `6aeae7ad fix: support boolean aggregate window functions`.
- Files touched: `server/ast/func_expr.go`, `server/analyzer/init.go`, and `server/expression/bool_window_agg.go`. Avoided alpha routine-default metadata files, epsilon array-dimensionality files, gamma logical-replication files, auditor materialized-view oracle files, dirty JSON/grant files, and alpha-owned full `./testing/go`.
- Red proof in clean detached verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-window-verify-sEJJLm` at committed `HEAD` `f8661c38`: focused `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestBooleanAggregatesCanBeWindowFunctionsRepro|TestAvgWindowAggregateFilterReturnsNullForEmptyFrameRepro|TestCumeDistWindowFunctionRepro|TestArrayAggOverArrayColumnReturnsHigherDimensionalArrayRepro)$' -count=1 -v` still failed four independent lanes. The bool aggregate lane failed with `an aggregation remained in the expression 'bool_or(items.paid) as bool_or OVER (...)' after analysis`.
- Root cause: GMS only sends names from its hardcoded window-function list through `buildWindowFunc`; `bool_or` and `bool_and` were treated as ordinary aggregate calls carrying an ignored `OVER` clause. The existing internal `count_distinct` hook is on that list.
- Fix: rewrite bool aggregate window calls through Doltgres's internal window hook with a marker argument, switch that hook to a dispatcher that preserves the existing array_agg window path, and add a bool window aggregate expression that evaluates each frame with PostgreSQL NULL-skipping semantics.
- Green proof in clean detached verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-boolwindow-green2-bVjeOG` at committed `HEAD` `2ee62c55` plus only the beta patch after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`, symlinked `third_party/dolt`, isolated cache (`GOCACHE=/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-boolwindow-gocache2-QCEHmR`, `GOTMPDIR=/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-boolwindow-gotmp2-kTblRX`), `GOFLAGS='-p=1'`, and explicit ICU flags:

  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestBooleanAggregatesCanBeWindowFunctionsRepro|TestArrayAggCanBeWindowFunctionRepro|TestAggregateFunctions|TestBoolAndInfersValuesBooleanTypeGuard)$' -count=1 -v`

### auditor - 2026-05-13 06:15 America/Phoenix

- Lane committed: extended the PostgreSQL oracle map for `TestMaterializedViewProbe` to include the fourth script, `ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW RENAME COLUMN preserves metadata and refresh`.
- Files touched: `server/node/alter_materialized_view.go`, `testing/go/materialized_view_probe_test.go`, `testing/go/testdata/postgres_oracle_migrations/materialized_view_probe_test.oracle-map.json`, and `testing/go/testdata/postgres_oracle_manifest.json`.
- Test-shape fix: made the fourth script schema-neutral for oracle generation by using `current_schema()` instead of hardcoded `public`, and normalized `pg_matviews.definition` whitespace/trailing semicolon like the earlier materialized-view oracle slices.
- Oracle/cache change: cached PostgreSQL rows and SQLSTATE for the rename-column script. PostgreSQL expects `pg_matviews.definition` to become `SELECT id, v AS amount FROM source`, and PostgreSQL accepts `ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW ordinary_table RENAME COLUMN v TO amount`.
- Confirmed stale handwritten expectation / implementation mismatch: the first live PostgreSQL refresh failed because the old test expected `ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW ordinary_table RENAME COLUMN ...` to error, but PostgreSQL succeeds. A clean verifier then showed Doltgres still returned `relation "ordinary_table" is not a materialized view` and also kept the stale materialized-view definition `SELECT id, v FROM source`.
- Fix: `ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW ... RENAME COLUMN` now delegates to `ALTER TABLE ... RENAME COLUMN` for ordinary tables, and for materialized views rewrites the stored definition's output alias before restoring the metadata comment.
- Pre-commit validation in clean detached verifier `/Users/ramazan/.cache/doltgresql-auditor-matview-rename-column-verify-cZRIy6` at committed `HEAD` `c215b453` plus only the auditor source/test/map/manifest files, after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`, symlinked `third_party/dolt`, isolated in-worktree cache, and explicit ICU flags:

- Root cause: Doltgres translated `array_agg` through an injected ordered-aggregate expression that cannot carry `OVER`, so GMS treated the query as a normal aggregate and rejected non-grouped columns.

Avoided beta-owned `server/logical_replication_changes.go`, epsilon array-agg/window files, auditor materialized-view oracle files, and dirty JSON/grant/RLS files.
- Red proof in clean verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-outargs-red-GihB2X` at committed `HEAD` before the alpha patch: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestCreateFunctionOutArgumentCatalogRepro|TestCreateProcedureOutArgumentCatalogRepro)$' -count=1 -v` failed because functions exposed `pronargs = 3` and `proargmodes = NULL` for `IN, OUT, OUT`, and procedures exposed `proargmodes = NULL` for `IN, OUT`.
- Root causes: `CREATE FUNCTION` did not persist argument modes, `pg_proc` hardcoded `proargmodes`/`proallargtypes` to `NULL`, and functions whose return type is derived from OUT/INOUT parameters still used `void` when no explicit `RETURNS` clause was present.
- Fix: persist function parameter modes, derive function return type from OUT/INOUT parameters when needed, render PostgreSQL-compatible `proargtypes`/`proallargtypes`/`proargmodes`/`provariadic` for user functions and procedures, and expose function modes through pg_get_function helper metadata.
- Green proof in clean detached verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-outargs-green2-Q5exHg` at current `HEAD` `d556b828` plus only the alpha patch after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`, symlinked `third_party/dolt`, isolated cache (`GOCACHE=/private/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-outargs-green2-gocache-61uZFo`, `GOTMPDIR=/private/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-outargs-green2-gotmp-0RWem6`), `GOFLAGS='-p=1'`, and explicit ICU flags:

  - `go test -vet=off ./core/functions ./server/ast ./server/node ./server/functions ./server/tables/pgcatalog -run '^$' -count=1`

- Fix: use `GetCharLengthFromTypmod(tm)` for `bpchar` string casts.

### beta - 2026-05-13 04:26 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete: minimal `xml` column persistence from `TestCommonBuiltinTypesRoundTripRepro/xml_column_round_trip`.
- Files committed: `testing/go/framework.go`. Shared `coop.md` and peer dirty files left unstaged.
- Red proof in clean verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-types-scan-HPTlEj` at `e475ee79`: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestCommonBuiltinTypesRoundTripRepro$' -count=1 -v` now passes through `polygon_column_round_trip`, then `xml_column_round_trip` returns `nil` XML values instead of `<doc><title>...</title></doc>`.
- Root cause: `pgx.Rows.Values()` uses pgx's XML codec `DecodeValue`, which unmarshals XML into `any` as nil even when the raw result bytes are present. The harness now preserves raw XML result bytes as strings only for non-NULL XML columns that pgx decoded as nil.
- Commit: `652a5fe8 test: preserve xml raw values in harness`.
- Focused proof in exact detached verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-beta-xml-post-vrtQGR` at `652a5fe8` after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`, symlinked `third_party/dolt`, isolated cache, and `GOFLAGS='-p=1'`:

- Lane committed: `eab1cc0d fix: support icu collation uniqueness`.
- Scope: minimal ICU `CREATE COLLATION` support and nondeterministic ICU unique equality for `TestIcuCollationProbe` and `TestIcuCollationUniqueConstraintUsesCollationEqualityRepro`.
- Files touched: `server/connection_handler.go`, `postgres/parser/sem/tree/create_table.go`, `server/node/citext_unique_table.go`, `testing/go/sqlstate_test.go`, and this coordination note only. Avoided dirty peer lanes in `server/ast/drop_schema.go`, `server/node/drop_schema.go`, `server/auth/default_privileges.go`, `server/functions/json.go`, and `server/node/grant.go`.
- Root cause: `CREATE COLLATION` was caught as an unsupported parser fallback, column parsing rejected user-defined collation identifiers before type resolution, and unique indexes over explicitly collated text still used native bytewise equality.
- Fix: accept `CREATE COLLATION ... (provider = icu, locale = ...)` as a no-row DDL compatibility statement, let SQL-identifier collation names pass column parsing, no-op `DROP COLLATION IF EXISTS`, and reuse the citext unique wrapper for explicit non-built-in collated text columns with case-insensitive equality.
- Shared checkout note: direct rerun was blocked by a peer dirty build error in `server/ast/drop_schema.go` (`undefined: vitess.DropDDLAction`), so alpha first verified in clean detached worktree `/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-collation-verify-0352` at `bdeb38a6` plus only alpha patch after `./postgres/parser/build.sh` and symlinking `third_party/dolt`.

### alpha - 2026-05-13 03:02 America/Phoenix

- Lane committed: `312c367b fix: preserve domain column defaults`.
- Expected files: `server/analyzer/domain_constraints.go` only, plus this coordination note. Avoiding active beta box type/function files, delta JSON, dirty grant/RLS, auditor oracle metadata, relation-size SQLSTATE, and gamma duplicate-target follow-up.
- Root cause confirmed in clean verifier `/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-domain-red-20260513-0252`: `loadDomainConstraints` overwrote an explicit column default with the domain type default, which is nil for domains without `DEFAULT`, so omitted inserts stored NULL instead of the column default.
- Fix: preserve an existing column default and only synthesize a domain default when the domain-typed column has no explicit/default expression.
- Red proof at `HEAD` `639ec944`: `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestDomainTypedColumnAcceptsValidColumnDefaultRepro$' -count=1 -v` failed with expected `{1,5}` vs actual `{1,NULL}`.
- Green proof in exact post-commit verifier `/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-domain-post-20260513-0300` at `312c367b`:
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestDomainTypedColumnAcceptsValidColumnDefaultRepro|TestAlterDomainSetDefaultAppliesToColumnsRepro|TestDomainDefaultFunctionEvaluatesOnInsertRepro|TestInsertSelectEnforcesDomainConstraintsGuard)$' -count=1 -v`
  - `go test -vet=off ./server/analyzer -run '^$' -count=1`
- Shared `coop.md` and peer dirty files left unstaged. Next alpha target: re-check current failures/active lanes before claiming another red.

### epsilon - 2026-05-13 02:50 America/Phoenix

- Lane committed: `e88faf7f fix: support insert delete returning aliases`.
- Scope: PostgreSQL 18 INSERT/DELETE `RETURNING old/new` aliases only. `old.col` on INSERT and `new.col` on DELETE are rewritten to NULL; the available alias is resolved to the affected row. UPDATE `old/new` remains a separate row-pair execution issue and was not claimed here.
- Red proof in clean verifier `/Users/ramazan/.cache/doltgresql-epsilon-pg-rewrite-red-20260513-0228` at current HEAD before the fix: `TestPostgres18InsertDeleteReturningOldNewAliasesRepro` failed with `table not found: old; relation does not exist`.
- Green proof in clean detached verifier `/Users/ramazan/.cache/doltgresql-epsilon-returning-alias-clean-20260513-0248` after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`:
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestPostgres18InsertDeleteReturningOldNewAliasesRepro$' -count=1 -v`
  - `go test -vet=off ./server/ast -run '^$' -count=1`
- No alpha-owned full `./testing/go` run was started by epsilon.

- Lane complete: PG16 plain-JSON lexical preservation oracle migration batch from `json_correctness_repro_test.go`.
- Files touched by auditor: `testing/go/gen_postgres_oracle_manifest.go`, `testing/go/postgres_oracle_manifest_test.go`, `testing/go/testdata/postgres_oracle_migrations/json_correctness_repro_test.oracle-map.json`, `testing/go/testdata/postgres_oracle_manifest.json`, and this coordination note. Avoided active beta network files, epsilon point files, gamma COPY files, alpha statement-atomicity files, delta JSON implementation files, and dirty `server/node/grant.go`.
- Artifact state: oracle artifacts are committed separately in `30ad93b1` (`test: add json lexical oracle batch`).
- Harness/cache root cause fixed: structural text values like `'[{"a": 1}]'::text` were incorrectly normalized as PostgreSQL arrays because bracket-array normalization ran on ordinary text. Added `oracle-selftest-text-values-that-look-like-arrays-stay-text` and limited bracket-array normalization to actual/explicit array mode. Refreshed the JSON map through `--refresh-oracle-map`; no hand-edited cached oracle output.
- Oracle/cache change: added 14 PostgreSQL-backed cached assertions for 8 plain-JSON lexical tests: extraction, `json_each`, `json_object_keys`, `json_array_elements`, `json_agg`/`json_object_agg`, `json_to_record`, and `json_populate_record`. Full PG16 JSON dry-run showed additional mismatch families, but this commit is limited to the lexical-preservation slice. PG18-only JSON tests remain unsuitable for the canonical PG16 oracle (`json_strip_nulls(json, boolean)` is missing in PG16).
- Post-commit exact verification at `30ad93b1` in clean verifier `/tmp/doltgresql-auditor-json-final-c8ffnR`: live PostgreSQL oracle run for the selftest plus JSON map passed; manifest schema/cache/generated/inventory guards passed; focused normal Doltgres run with the 8 migrated JSON tests and `-count=2` failed each migrated test twice as expected. Count=2 log: `/tmp/doltgresql-auditor-json-final-count2.log`.
- Live PostgreSQL validation in clean verifier `/tmp/doltgresql-auditor-json-YLhhyM` at base `fe597633` plus auditor patch after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`:
  - `CGO_CPPFLAGS='-I/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/include' CGO_LDFLAGS='-L/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib' PKG_CONFIG_PATH='/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib/pkgconfig' DOLTGRES_ORACLE=1 go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestPostgresOracleManifest$/(oracle-selftest-text-values-that-look-like-arrays-stay-text|json-correctness-repro-test)' -count=1 -v`
  - `CGO_CPPFLAGS='-I/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/include' CGO_LDFLAGS='-L/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib' PKG_CONFIG_PATH='/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib/pkgconfig' go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestPostgresOracleManifestSchema|TestPostgresOracleCacheCoversManifestScriptEntries|TestPostgresOracleManifestGenerated|TestPostgresOracleManifestInventory)$' -count=1 -v`
- Confirmed mismatches, all reproduced consistently with `CGO_CPPFLAGS='-I/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/include' CGO_LDFLAGS='-L/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib' PKG_CONFIG_PATH='/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib/pkgconfig' go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestJsonExtractionPreservesJsonSubdocumentTextRepro|TestJsonExtractionPreservesJsonObjectKeyOrderRepro|TestJsonEachPreservesJsonObjectOrderAndDuplicatesRepro|TestJsonObjectKeysPreservesJsonObjectOrderAndDuplicatesRepro|TestJsonArrayElementsPreservesJsonElementTextRepro|TestJsonAggregatesPreserveJsonInputTextRepro|TestJsonToRecordPreservesNestedJsonTextRepro|TestJsonPopulateRecordPreservesNestedJsonTextRepro)$' -count=2 -v`:
  - `TestJsonExtractionPreservesJsonSubdocumentTextRepro`: PostgreSQL cache expects extracted plain `json` subdocuments to preserve whitespace (`{ "b" : 1, "c" : 2 }`); Doltgres canonicalizes to `{"b": 1, "c": 2}`. Suspected area: plain-json storage/extraction currently loses lexical text.
  - `TestJsonExtractionPreservesJsonObjectKeyOrderRepro`: PostgreSQL cache expects `{"b":1,"a":2}`; Doltgres returns sorted/canonicalized `{"a": 2, "b": 1}`. Suspected area: plain-json object order preservation.
  - `TestJsonEachPreservesJsonObjectOrderAndDuplicatesRepro`: PostgreSQL cache expects `b:1,a:2` and `a:1,a:2`; Doltgres returns `a:2,b:1` and `a:2`. Suspected area: `json_each` using JSONB-like map semantics for plain JSON.
  - `TestJsonObjectKeysPreservesJsonObjectOrderAndDuplicatesRepro`: PostgreSQL cache expects `b,a` and `a,a`; Doltgres returns sorted/deduplicated keys. Suspected area: `json_object_keys` plain-json parser representation.
  - `TestJsonArrayElementsPreservesJsonElementTextRepro`: PostgreSQL cache expects `{"b":1,"a":2}|{"a":1,"a":2}|{ "c" : 3 }`; Doltgres returns canonicalized/deduplicated elements like `{"a": 2, "b": 1}|{"a": 2}|{"c": 3}`. Suspected area: `json_array_elements*` plain-json element serialization.
  - `TestJsonAggregatesPreserveJsonInputTextRepro`: PostgreSQL cache expects `json_agg`/`json_object_agg` to retain input plain-json text and duplicate keys; Doltgres canonicalizes/deduplicates nested objects. Suspected area: JSON aggregate transition/output for plain `json`.
  - `TestJsonToRecordPreservesNestedJsonTextRepro`: PostgreSQL cache expects nested plain-json fields `{"b":1,"a":2}` and `{"a":1,"a":2}`; Doltgres returns `{"a":2,"b":1}` and `{"a":2}`. Suspected area: `json_to_record` plain-json field conversion.
  - `TestJsonPopulateRecordPreservesNestedJsonTextRepro`: PostgreSQL cache expects nested plain-json field text, but Doltgres errors `function: 'json_populate_record' not found; function does not exist` (`SQLSTATE XX000`). Suspected area: missing `json_populate_record` implementation.
- Next target for auditor: either continue JSON with the non-scalar-key / JSONPath / JSONB error-boundary mismatch families from the full PG16 dry run, or move to another unclaimed generated oracle candidate after re-checking active lanes.

### alpha - 2026-05-13 01:46 America/Phoenix

- Lane active: PL/pgSQL function side effects must roll back when the outer DML later fails constraints/FKs, starting from `TestFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnOuterStatementErrorRepro` and adjacent statement-atomicity repros in `testing/go/statement_atomicity_repro_test.go`.
- Expected files: likely statement transaction/finalizer or function execution plumbing only, especially `server/node/context_root_finalizer.go` / PL/pgSQL framework if proven. Avoiding active beta inet files, epsilon parser/check-constraint files, auditor oracle artifacts, dirty `server/node/grant.go`, and peer full-suite work.
- Next action: reproduce the focused repros in a clean detached verifier at current `HEAD`, then fix the root cause without touching peer lanes.
- Red proof in clean verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-stmt-rollback-20260513-0146` at `b9006b56` after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`: focused run of `TestFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnOuterStatementErrorRepro`, `TestInsertFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnForeignKeyErrorRepro`, and `TestUpdateFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnConstraintErrorRepro` fails because target rows roll back but PL/pgSQL audit rows remain at count `1`.
- Root cause: the previous statement-savepoint fix created the internal savepoint in `ContextRootFinalizer.Next`. These DML paths can evaluate PL/pgSQL expressions while building the child iterator, before `Next`, so function side effects happen outside the statement savepoint.
- Additional root cause: OK/empty/max1 result spooling returned immediately when `iter.Next` produced an error, so `ContextRootFinalizer.Close` did not run for non-RETURNING DML errors and the statement savepoint was left unresolved.
- Fix: `ContextRootFinalizer` now creates the internal statement savepoint before building its child iterator, and the Doltgres result helpers always close iterators on error while preserving the original query error.
- Focused green in clean verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-stmt-rollback-20260513-0146` with the final narrow patch:
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnOuterStatementErrorRepro|TestInsertFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnForeignKeyErrorRepro|TestUpdateFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnConstraintErrorRepro)$' -count=1 -v`
- Broader statement-atomicity sweep green in the same verifier:
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestInsertSelectFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnConstraintErrorRepro|TestInsertSelectFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnDuplicateKeyErrorRepro|TestOnConflictDoNothingFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnNonTargetUniqueErrorRepro|TestOnConflictDoUpdateFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnConstraintErrorRepro|TestOnConflictDoUpdateInsertFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnConstraintErrorRepro|TestFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnOuterStatementErrorRepro|TestInsertFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnForeignKeyErrorRepro|TestUpdateFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnConstraintErrorRepro|TestSelectFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnExpressionErrorRepro|TestReturningFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnExpressionErrorRepro)$' -count=1 -v`
  - `go test -vet=off ./server ./server/node -run '^$' -count=1`
- Next action: commit only `server/node/context_root_finalizer.go` and `server/doltgres_handler.go`; leave shared `coop.md` and peer dirty files unstaged.

Avoiding active beta point type/operator files, alpha INSERT RETURNING atomicity files, epsilon parser/check-constraint files, auditor oracle artifacts/manifest, dirty `server/node/grant.go`, and peer staged parser files.
- Red proof in clean verifier `/tmp/doltgresql-delta-publication` at `62c821a6`: `ALTER PUBLICATION pub_filter_pub ADD TABLES IN SCHEMA pub_filter_aux;` succeeds, but the cached PostgreSQL oracle expects SQLSTATE `22023` with `cannot add schema to publication`.
- Fix: reject schema publication membership when any table member has a column list or row filter, preserving PostgreSQL's distinct messages for adding schemas to an already restricted publication versus adding restricted tables to a schema publication. Allowed `InvalidParameterValue` through the PostgreSQL wire-error path so the cached oracle sees SQLSTATE `22023`.

- Starting point: adjacent verifier from alpha's prior DELETE RETURNING lane showed the test still red: `INSERT ... RETURNING audit_and_return_bad_returning(id), 1 / 0` returns the expected division error but leaves both the target row and audit side-effect row committed. I will reproduce in a clean detached verifier before editing.
- Red proof in clean verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-insert-returning-20260513-0121` at `8dcd3e5b`: focused `TestReturningFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnExpressionErrorRepro` fails exactly the same way. The INSERT returns `division by zero`, but `returning_function_target` and `returning_function_audit` each have count `1` instead of `0`.
- Root cause/fix: `ContextRootFinalizer` noticed row-iterator errors but only cleared Doltgres context state. It did not roll the SQL transaction back before the autocommit iterator closed, so writes already applied by INSERT RETURNING and PL/pgSQL side-effect statements survived. Added an internal statement savepoint around root execution; on iterator/flush/finalizer errors it rolls back and releases that savepoint before autocommit can close.
- Focused green in the clean verifier:
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestReturningFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnExpressionErrorRepro$' -count=1 -v`
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestSelectFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnExpressionErrorRepro|TestReturningFunctionSideEffectsRollBackOnExpressionErrorRepro|TestUpdateReturningExpressionErrorRollsBackRowChangeRepro|TestDeleteReturningExpressionErrorRollsBackDeleteRepro|TestUpdateReturningFunctionSucceedsAfterRowChangeRepro|TestDeleteReturningFunctionSucceedsAfterDeleteRepro|TestOnConflictDoUpdateReturningExpressionErrorRollsBackUpdateRepro|TestOnConflictDoUpdateReturningFunctionSucceedsAfterUpdateRepro)$' -count=1 -v`
  - `go test -vet=off ./server/node -run '^$' -count=1`
- Adjacent broader side-effect sweep still has separate existing reds in INSERT/ON CONFLICT/UPDATE constraint-error cases where function side-effect audit rows survive. Leaving those as separate follow-up lanes; this slice is limited to the RETURNING expression-error failure.

### beta - 2026-05-13 00:55 America/Phoenix

- Lane active: rejected `DROP DOMAIN` dependency checks corrupt subsequent table expression evaluation in `TestDropDomainUsedByCheckConstraintRequiresCascadeRepro`, `TestDropDomainUsedByGeneratedColumnRequiresCascadeRepro`, and `TestDropDomainUsedByExpressionIndexRequiresCascadeRepro`.
- Expected files: `server/analyzer/domain_constraints.go` and `server/expression/explicit_cast.go` only. Avoiding active alpha subscription files, epsilon `server/connection_handler.go` view-DML lane, auditor oracle artifacts, delta `server/functions/to_char.go`, gamma parser/AST work, dirty grant files, and active manifest ownership.
- Red proof: focused domain dependency sweep shows view/function/default/schema-qualified/no-dependent cascade cases pass, but after a rejected drop on domains referenced by check constraints, generated columns, or expression indexes, the table becomes unusable and inserts fail with `unable to find field with index 1 in row of 1 columns`.
- Root cause: domain check constraints attached to `ExplicitCast` were rewriting `VALUE` to the cast child expression. When the cast lived inside a table check, generated column, or expression-index hidden column, the stored domain check referenced the outer table row field (for example field 1) but `ExplicitCast.Eval` evaluates domain checks against a one-column row containing only the cast result.
- Expanded root cause: expression-index hidden columns also exposed a nested cast problem: generated expressions can rehydrate the inner `amount::domain` cast with an unresolved domain target, so the outer `::integer` looked up a cast from that unresolved domain placeholder instead of the domain's base type.
- Fix: rewrite cast-domain `VALUE` checks to `GetField(0)` over the cast result's base type instead of the original child expression, and resolve plus unwrap domain source types before explicit-cast lookup.
- Focused green:
  - `CGO_CPPFLAGS='-I/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/include' CGO_LDFLAGS='-L/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib' PKG_CONFIG_PATH='/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib/pkgconfig' GOCACHE=/tmp/doltgresql-beta-gocache GOTMPDIR=/tmp/doltgresql-beta-gotmp go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestDropDomainUsedByExpressionIndexRequiresCascadeRepro$' -count=1 -v`
  - `CGO_CPPFLAGS='-I/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/include' CGO_LDFLAGS='-L/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib' PKG_CONFIG_PATH='/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib/pkgconfig' GOCACHE=/tmp/doltgresql-beta-gocache GOTMPDIR=/tmp/doltgresql-beta-gotmp go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestDropDomainUsedByCheckConstraintRequiresCascadeRepro|TestDropDomainUsedByGeneratedColumnRequiresCascadeRepro|TestDropDomainUsedByExpressionIndexRequiresCascadeRepro|TestDropDomainUsedByViewRequiresCascadeRepro|TestDropDomainUsedByFunctionRequiresCascadeRepro|TestDropDomainUsedByColumnDefaultRequiresCascadeRepro|TestDropDomainDependencyChecksSchemaQualifiedDomainRepro|TestDropDomainCascadeWithoutDependentsRepro)$' -count=1 -v`

### beta - 2026-05-13 00:49 America/Phoenix

- Lane active: `to_date` / `to_timestamp` input-format correctness in `server/functions/formatting.go`.
- Expected files: `server/functions/formatting.go` only unless focused validation shows a test harness issue. Avoiding active alpha subscription files, gamma parser/AST work, delta extension files, dirty grant files, auditor oracle artifacts, and untracked bit-string oracle artifacts.
- Status: initial focused repro command failed before execution because beta-private `GOTMPDIR` did not exist; created `/tmp/doltgresql-beta-gotmp` and `/tmp/doltgresql-beta-gocache`.
- Red proof: focused repros failed as expected; output-only `OF` returned nil error for both input functions, and `IYYYIWID` with `ID=1` resolved to `2006-01-01` instead of PostgreSQL's Monday `2005-12-26`.
- Root cause: `getDateTimeFromFormat` treated `OF` as an input timezone token even though PostgreSQL only allows it in `to_char`, and the `ID` parser failed to convert ISO Monday=1..Sunday=7 into the internal Sunday=1 weekday numbering used by `isoWeekDate2Date`.
- Fix: `server/functions/formatting.go` now rejects `OF` in input parsing with a feature-not-supported-mapped error and maps ISO `ID` values to the internal weekday numbering before ISO week-date resolution.
- Focused green:
  - `CGO_CPPFLAGS='-I/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/include' CGO_LDFLAGS='-L/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib' PKG_CONFIG_PATH='/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib/pkgconfig' GOCACHE=/tmp/doltgresql-beta-gocache GOTMPDIR=/tmp/doltgresql-beta-gotmp go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestToDateRejectsOutputOnlyOFPatternRepro|TestToDateIsoWeekdayParsesMondayRepro|TestToTimestampRejectsOutputOnlyOFPatternRepro|TestToTimestampIsoWeekdayParsesMondayRepro)$' -count=1 -v`

- Confirmed mismatch: `TestToCharFractionalSecondPrecisionTokensRepro`. PostgreSQL `FF1 FF2 FF3 FF4 FF5 FF6` is `1 12 123 1234 12345 123456`; Doltgres returns `3 23 123 0123 00123 123456`.
- Confirmed mismatch: `TestToCharFirstMonthAndWeekdayNamesRepro`. PostgreSQL renders month and weekday names for `MONTH Month month MON Mon mon MM DAY Day day DY Dy dy D`; Doltgres emits only the numeric pieces (`      01       1`).
- Confirmed mismatch: `TestToCharOrdinalSuffixTeenDatesRepro`. PostgreSQL uses `11TH 11th`, `12TH 12th`, and `13TH 13th`; Doltgres returns `11ST 11st`, `12ND 12nd`, and `13RD 13rd`.
- Confirmed mismatch: `TestToCharTimezoneFieldsMatchPostgresSessionZoneRepro`. For `timestamp` without time zone under `America/New_York`, PostgreSQL emits blank `TZ` with `+00`; Doltgres emits `AMERICA/NEW_YORK -04:00`. For `timestamptz`, PostgreSQL emits `EDT -04`; Doltgres emits `AMERICA/NEW_YORK -04:00`.
- Confirmed mismatch: `TestToCharNumericFormatsPostgresPatternsRepro`. PostgreSQL returns `1,234.50` and ` -42.5`; Doltgres errors with `to_char(numeric,text) is not supported yet`.
- Repro confirmed twice with auditor cache: `CGO_CPPFLAGS='-I/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/include' CGO_LDFLAGS='-L/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib' PKG_CONFIG_PATH='/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib/pkgconfig' GOCACHE=/tmp/doltgresql-auditor-gocache GOTMPDIR=/tmp/doltgresql-auditor-gotmp go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestToChar' -count=2 -v`.
- Suspected areas: interval duration unit/fraction handling, `FFn` precision slicing, month/weekday name tokens, ordinal suffix teen special cases, timezone abbreviation/offset semantics, and missing `to_char(numeric,text)`.

### alpha - 2026-05-13 00:38 America/Phoenix

- Lane: `ALTER SUBSCRIPTION ... ADD/SET/DROP PUBLICATION` implicit refresh handling from auditor-confirmed `TestSubscriptionAddPublicationRefreshRejectedWhenDisabledRepro`.
- Expected files: `server/node/publication_subscription.go` and stale expectation cleanup in `testing/go/publication_subscription_test.go` only. Avoiding active delta CREATE DATABASE TEMPLATE, gamma DELETE USING parser/AST, auditor oracle artifacts, dirty grant/RLS files, and full-suite runner.
- Red proof from auditor: disabled subscription accepted `ALTER SUBSCRIPTION ... ADD PUBLICATION ... WITH (copy_data = false)` even though PostgreSQL treats refresh as true by default and returns SQLSTATE `55000`.
- Plan: validate ADD/SET/DROP publication refresh options before mutating subscription membership, require `refresh=false` for disabled metadata-only changes, preserve existing refresh=false allowed tests, and keep invalid `copy_data` from partially mutating metadata.
- Expanded root cause: `core/subscriptions` sorted publication names during normalization and the ALTER ADD/DROP paths matched names case-insensitively, so `subpublications` order and quoted-name case semantics diverged from PostgreSQL. Alpha is adding `core/subscriptions/collection.go` to this lane for the metadata-order fix.
- SQLSTATE note: `pgcode.ObjectNotInPrerequisiteState` from the node layer was still being cast through `sql.CastSQLError`, so alpha is also touching `server/doltgres_handler.go` to preserve SQLSTATE `55000` on the wire.
- Fix: publication ADD/SET/DROP validates refresh/copy_data before mutating membership, disabled implicit refresh now returns SQLSTATE `55000`, subscription publication lists compact exact duplicates without sorting, and ADD/DROP membership checks are case-sensitive.
- Clean verifier `/private/tmp/doltgresql-alpha-subscription-refresh-20260513-0108` green after `./postgres/parser/build.sh`:
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestSubscriptionAddPublicationRefreshRejectedWhenDisabledRepro|TestSubscriptionAddPublicationRefreshFalseUpdatesCatalog|TestSubscriptionAddPublicationNamesAreCaseSensitiveRepro|TestSubscriptionAddPublicationRefreshFalseRepro|TestSubscriptionSetPublicationRefreshFalseRepro|TestSubscriptionDropPublicationRefreshFalseRepro|TestSubscriptionAddPublicationRejectsInvalidCopyDataRepro|TestSubscriptionSetPublicationRejectsInvalidCopyDataRepro|TestSubscriptionDropPublicationRejectsInvalidCopyDataRepro|TestSubscriptionDDLAndCatalogs)$' -count=1 -v`

- Root cause: Doltgres rejected the syntax before planning; GMS single-table truncate then rejects referenced parents based on FK metadata unless FK checks are disabled.

### epsilon - 2026-05-13 00:22 America/Phoenix

- Lane committed: `2cb732ec fix: preserve default database public privileges`.
- Initial red while checking `TestDropLanguageRequiresOwnershipRepro`: the non-owner connection failed at startup with `permission denied for database postgres` before reaching the expected language ownership check.
- Root cause: `f619a852` made startup enforce database `CONNECT`, but database default `PUBLIC` privileges were not honored unless they had explicit ACL rows.
- Fix: `HasDatabasePrivilege` now honors default `PUBLIC` database privileges when no explicit public ACL exists, and `REVOKE` materializes the default ACL before removing a default privilege so revoked `CONNECT`/`TEMPORARY` remain revoked.
- Added coverage for default `PUBLIC CONNECT`.

### epsilon - 2026-05-13 00:20 America/Phoenix

- Lane committed: `f619a852 fix: enforce database connect privilege`.
- Root cause: startup selected the requested database but never checked database `CONNECT`, so revoking `CONNECT` from `PUBLIC` did not prevent a normal role from opening a new session.
- Fix: startup now checks role/group/public database `CONNECT` after successful database selection and returns FATAL `42501` on denial; generic auth also maps `AuthType_CONNECT` to database `CONNECT`.

- Repro confirmed twice with omega cache: `CGO_CPPFLAGS=-I/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/include CGO_LDFLAGS=-L/opt/homebrew/opt/icu4c@78/lib GOCACHE=/tmp/doltgresql-omega-gocache GOTMPDIR=/tmp/doltgresql-omega-gotmp go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestPublicationRejectsSchemaAddAfterColumnListOrFilterRepro$' -count=1 -v`. ### alpha - 2026-05-12 16:08 America/Phoenix

- Lane complete: `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES` now persists default ACL metadata, exposes it through `pg_default_acl`, and applies matching default grants to future tables, sequences, and routines. Added parser support for PostgreSQL 18 `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES ... ON LARGE OBJECTS`.

### alpha - 2026-05-12 19:11 America/Phoenix

- Lane committed: `97f31121 fix: release xact advisory locks via connection engine`.
- Root cause for latest alpha full-run abort: transaction-scope advisory cleanup called `functions.ReleaseSessionXactLocks`, which used the process-global running server and could panic when the global server pointer was nil while cleanup ran. The full run then saw `unexpected EOF` / pgx close panic in `TestAdvisoryLockConcurrency`.
- Fix: connection cleanup now passes the active handler engine's `LockSubsystem` into `ReleaseSessionXactLocksWithSubsystem`; `getLockSubsystem` also nil-checks the process-global server for legacy function paths.
- Green focused proof:
  - `go test -vet=off ./server/functions -run 'TestReleaseSessionXactLocks' -count=1 -v`
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestAdvisoryLockConcurrency$' -count=1 -v`
  - `go test -vet=off ./server ./server/functions -run 'TestReleaseSessionXactLocks|^$' -count=1`

### alpha - 2026-05-12 19:31 America/Phoenix

- Lane committed: `4d6978a6 fix: require select privileges for dml reads`.
- Root cause: DML statements carried one write auth check, so expressions in `RETURNING`, `WHERE`, `SET`, and `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` could read ungranted columns. Added supplemental auth checks on Vitess auth info and attach column-level SELECT / ON CONFLICT UPDATE checks from the AST.
- Focused proof:
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^(TestColumnInsertReturningRequiresSelectPrivilegeGuard|TestColumnUpdateGrantRequiresSelectForSourceColumnsRepro|TestColumnUpdateRequiresSelectForWhereColumnsRepro|TestColumnOnConflictUpdateRequiresSelectForSourceColumnsRepro|TestColumnOnConflictUpdateRequiresSelectForWhereColumnsRepro|TestColumnOnConflictUpdateRequiresUpdatePrivilegeRepro|TestColumnDeleteRequiresSelectForWhereColumnsRepro|TestColumnDeleteReturningRequiresSelectPrivilegeRepro|TestColumnUpdateReturningRequiresSelectPrivilegeGuard)$' -count=1 -v`
  - `go test -vet=off ./server/ast ./server/auth -run 'ColumnAuth|AdditionalAuth|^$' -count=1`
- Broader `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestColumn' -count=1 -v` now keeps the privilege cases green but still fails on unrelated `TestColumnDefaultSequenceDependencyPopulatesPgDependRepro`.

### alpha - 2026-05-12 19:36 America/Phoenix

### epsilon - 2026-05-12 19:25 America/Phoenix

- Lane committed: `37c95dde fix: support create table as execute`.
- Source touched: `postgres/parser/parser/sql.y`, `postgres/parser/sem/tree/create_table.go`, `postgres/parser/sem/tree/walk.go`, `server/ast/create_table.go`, `server/connection_handler.go`, `server/node/create_table_as_execute.go`.
- Root cause: parser only accepted `select_stmt` after `CREATE TABLE ... AS`; `EXECUTE prepared_stmt(...)` failed before execution. The fix parses CTAS AS EXECUTE, expands it through the connection handler using the prepared statement's stored query and bind variables, then runs the normal CTAS path so permissions and CTAS schema rules still apply.
- Green proof in clean detached worktree at base `b9539c2d` plus epsilon patch, with copied private Go 1.26.2 GOROOT and generated parser artifacts:
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestCreateTableAsExecutePreparedStatementRepro$' -count=1 -v`
- Epsilon did not start a full `./testing/go` run; alpha remains full-suite owner.

### gamma - 2026-05-12 19:34 America/Phoenix

- Lane committed: `f138137e fix: expose pg_attribute physical metadata`, touching only `server/tables/pgcatalog/pg_attribute.go`.
- Root cause: `pg_attribute` rows hard-coded `attlen=0`, `attbyval=false`, `attalign='i'`, and defaulted `attstorage='p'`, so type-specific physical metadata was missing for fixed-width, varlena, bool, numeric, and timestamp columns.
- Green proof in clean detached verifier at committed HEAD `4d6978a6` plus gamma patch, with parser artifacts regenerated and gamma-only clean caches:
  - `go test -vet=off ./testing/go -run '^TestPgAttributePhysicalTypeMetadataRepro$' -count=1 -v`
